calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Grant Cross Petitioners Motion To Vacate, Remand And Or Modify Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Aug-30-2016 Advanced From Nov-10-16 To Sep-22-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Grant Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Hearing held - see word minutes for this date

Motion For Stay Of Proceedings Pending Further Orders And Adjudications Under Faa In Federal District Court Of Maryland
Off Calendar Oct-24-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeffrey Bornstein of Rosen Bien Galvan & Grunfeld LLP telephone number 415-433-6830 for Petitioner Square Inc.
Defendant Micheal Wein telephone number 301-441-1151 appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT MICHAEL WEIN'S Motion For Stay Of Proceedings Pending Further Orders And Adjudications Under FAA In Federal District Court Of Maryland is denied. Paragraph 51 of the parties' agreement contains a forum selection clause that provides for judicial resolution of the parties' dispute in San Francisco. A court will enforce such a clause unless the opposing party demonstrates "that the contractually selected forum would be unavailable or unable to accomplish substantial justice or that no rational basis exists for the choice of forum." (Intershop Communications v. Sup. Crt. (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 191, 199.) Mr. Wein fails to meet that heavy burden. Square, Inc. states that it is headquartered in San Francisco and therefore there is a reasonable basis for a San Francisco forum. This court has jurisdiction to resolve the issues raised by Square, Inc.'s petition, is able to resolve all issues of federal law, has no bias for or against either side, has sufficient resources to resolve all issues raised by the petition, perceives no reason in law or equity why it should not resolve those issues, notwithstanding an essentially identical lawsuit currently pending in the United States District Court for the District of Maryland. Nothing in the FAA bars this court from resolving the issues raised by the parties. Indeed, petitions to confirm and vacate awards per the FAA are routinely heard in this court. It appears that Mr. Wein now favors having the United States District Court for the Northern District of California hear this case. However, Mr. Wein had the right to remove this case to the Northern District and failed to do so. At Mr. Wein's request, a research attorney of this court contacted a law clerk of Judge Grimm. The law clerk of this court stated, after speaking with Judge Grimm's law clerk that "Judge Grimm agrees that coordination is not necessary and believes that since the matter was filed in SF Superior first, there's no reason the matter shouldn't proceed here [in San Francisco] according to the current schedule. Judge Grimm appreciated the courtesy call." Notwithstanding the foregoing statement attributed to Judge Grimm, this court's ruling is not based on the so-called first case to be filed rule. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
